title: Stemodia subhastata
taxonomy: Stemodia subhastata belongs to the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as broomrape or Indian pipe family. This family is a group of flowering plants that are parasitic or semi-parasitic in nature. The species Stemodia subhastata is native to certain tropical and subtropical regions. intro: Stemodia subhastata, also known as Chay-Chen, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Plantaginaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory infections.
